Name: Alakazam
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, instead of Alakazam’s normal attack, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks. Alakazam copies that attack including its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Alakazam’s type is still Psychic.) This power can’t be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Alakazam and the Defending Pokémon don’t have the same number of Energy cards attached to them, this attack’s base damage is 20 instead of 80.

Name: Ampharos
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon and attach it to your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Ampharos is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard all [L] Energy cards attached to Ampharos. If you do, this attack’s base damage is 80 instead of 40.

Name: Arbok
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Blastoise
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Blastoise is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to Blastoise, if any. Then, if there are any Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon, choose 1 of them and discard it. This power can’t be used if Blastoise is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy Cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.

Name: Butterfree
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Butterfree is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from Butterfree.

Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon into [R] Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can’t be used if Charizard is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If 1 of them is tails, discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard. If both are tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Charizard.

Name: Clefable
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a card from your hand back on your deck. If you do, search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Clefable is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 times the number of heads.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Dragonite
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dragonite is on your Bench, you may reduce your Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost to 0.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Fearow
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Feraligatr
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Feraligatr is your Active Pokémon and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. Either way, if you have any Benched Pokémon, switch Feraligatr with 1 of them. This power can’t be used if Feraligatr is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If here are no damage counters on the Defending Pokémon, this attack’s base damage is 40 instead of 70.

Name: Gengar
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prizes, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s) to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can’t be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Switch Gengar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

Name: Golem
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All damage done by attacks to Golem is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Don’t apply Resistance.

Name: Kingler
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Machamp
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and rearrange them as you like. This power can’t be used if Machamp is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Count the number of Pokémon you have in play with damage counters on them. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of those Pokémon.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any. This power can’t be used if Meganium is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Mew
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Count the number of Energy cards attached to that Pokémon. Put that many damage counters on the Pokémon.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Poliwrath
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Poliwrath is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, take all Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon, if any, and attach them to Poliwrath. Then switch Poliwrath with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of [W] Energy attached to Poliwrath. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

Name: Bill’s Maintenance
Description: If you have any cards in your hand, shuffle 1 of them into your deck, then draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, count the number of cards in your opponent’s hand and draw that many cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Dual Ball
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card other than a Baby Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Energy Removal 2
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and discard it.

Name: Energy Restore
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don’t have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

Name: Mary’s Impulse
Description: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw 2 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Master Ball
Description: Look at 7 cards from the top of your deck. You may choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from those cards, show it your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the rest into your deck.

Name: Multi Technical Machine 01
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Multi Technical Machine 01.

Name: Pokémon Nurse
Description: Remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon. Then discard all Energy cards attached to it, if any.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Pokémon Reversal
Description: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Flip a coin. If heads, switch that Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Power Charge
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, shuffle 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck (1 if you have only 1).

Name: Professor Elm’s Training Method
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Professor Oak’s Research
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Strength Charm
Move-Energy: Whenever an attack from the Pokémon that Strength Charm is attached to does damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage. At the end of your turn in which this happens, discard Strength Charm.
Description: Attach Strength Charm to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

Name: Warp Point
Description: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any; then you switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

Name: Moo-Moo Milk
Description: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. Remove 2 damage counters times the number of heads from that Pokémon. If the Pokémon has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

Name: Potion
Description: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (1 if that Pokémon has only 1).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to does damage with an attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage. At the end of every turn, put 1 damage counter on the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to, unless it’s [D] or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Metal Energy
Description:
Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to isn’t [M], whenever it damages a Pokémon by an attack, reduce that damage by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)
